notes: Verified via WebSearch 2026-07-11 (Wikipedia, Canada.ca, arabz.ca, ayam-alotal.com): July 1, 1867 Confederation (British North America Act) uniting the Province of Canada (Ontario/Quebec), Nova Scotia, and New Brunswick. Named Dominion Day from its 1879 statutory establishment until renamed Canada Day in 1982 (same year as the Canada Act patriation).
Federal statutory holiday across all provinces/territories: banks, post offices, government offices, schools closed. If July 1 falls on a Sunday, the statutory holiday moves to the following Monday.
Evening fireworks in 15+ major Canadian cities, most notably Ottawa — this practical detail is absent from the Arabic competitor content reviewed.
Deliberately did not conflate with US Independence Day (July 4) despite the close dates; content covers only Canada Day facts.
Global/diaspora event (_countryCode: ca), aliasSlugs: [] — same pattern as thanksgiving-usa/christmas-usa.
